zh.lifeSummary: Joan Fuller於1574年出生在英國英格蘭奧可福爾卡維沙安。她於1622年8月22日與John Deane在英國奧可福爾卡維沙安結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1670年1月25日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可沃特托享年96歲。

nameMeanings.name: Fuller
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a dresser of cloth, from Middle English fuller ‘fuller of cloth’ (partly from Old English fullere, partly from Old French fouleor, foleur, Latin fullo). Raw cloth had to be fulled, i.e. scoured, cleansed, and thickened by beating or trampling it in water, a process also known as walking or tucking, hence the surnames Walker and Tucker alongside Fuller. These three terms and surnames are characteristic of different parts of England. In general, in Middle English, Fuller is southern and eastern, while Walker belongs to the west and north and Tucker is southwestern. Compare Fullen .
English: variant of Fullard with loss of -d.
German (Füller): occupational name for a person whose work involved filling, such as a dauber, or a nickname for a gourmand or glutton. Compare Filler .

variants.name: Walker
meanings: English (mainly North and Midlands) and Scottish: occupational name for a fuller, from Middle English walker, Old English wealcere (an agent derivative of wealcan ‘to walk, tread’), ‘one who trampled cloth in a bath of lye or kneaded it, in order to strengthen it’. This was the regular term for the occupation during the Middle Ages in western and northern England. Compare Fuller and Tucker . As a Scottish surname it has also been used as a translation of Gaelic Mac an Fhucadair ‘son of the fuller’. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

History: The name was brought to North America from northern England and Scotland independently by many different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. Samuel Walker came to Lynn, MA, c. 1630; Philip Walker was in Rehoboth, MA, in or before 1643. The surname was also established in VA before 1650; a Thomas Walker, born in 1715 in King and Queen County, VA, was a physician, soldier, and explorer.

variants.name: Filler
meanings: South German: occupational name for a flayer or skinner, from Middle High German viller ‘knacker’, from villen ‘to skin’.

variants.name: Fullen
meanings: English: occupational name for a fuller (see Fuller ), from Old French fulun ‘fuller’.
English (Lancashire): branch of this family in Lancashire probably represents a variant of Fulham (see Fullam ), reintroduced from Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: Joan Fuller was born in 1574, in Caversham, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. She married John Deane on 22 August 1622, in Caversham, Oxfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 25 January 1670, in Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 96.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Fuller(은)는 1574년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 카버샤에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1622년 8월 22일 영국 스퍼드실르 카버샤에서 John Deane(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1670년 1월 25일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 왓르타운에서 96세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan Fuller родилась в 1574 г. в местности кавиршам, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Deane 22 августа 1622 г. в местности кавиршам, оксфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 25 января 1670 г. в местности вэитиртоун, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 96 лет.
